description:
The image displays three panels of Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) data products. Panel (a) shows an InSAR interferogram acquired between 01 August 1995 and 02 April 1996, with a normal baseline (B_n) of 44 metres and a temporal baseline (T) of 245 days. The plot axes are labelled 'North Direction - Step 125 [m]' and 'East Direction - Step 125 [m]', indicating spatial units in metres at 125-meter steps. A compass rose points North. The image displays concentric fringe patterns, characteristic of phase variations due to ground motion or topography, processed as part of a phase unwrapping procedure.

Panel (b) presents two distinct satellite imagery products. The left image, labelled 'Amplitude', shows a greyscale representation of radar signal amplitude over a landscape featuring urban areas, a river system, and general land cover. The right image, labelled 'Interferogram', shows mottled greyscale patterns representing InSAR phase data. Both images in panel (b) include directional labels for 'azimuth' (horizontal) and 'range' (vertical), and a compass rose indicating North. A scale bar on the 'Interferogram' image indicates a length of 50 km.
